content.text:
The release pipeline fails because we did a recent change, and now apparently Go Releaser cannot work anymore. What is the proper fix?  GoReleaser v2.3.2 installed successfully
Using src as working directory
C:\hostedtoolcache\windows\goreleaser-action\2.3.2\x64\goreleaser.exe release --clean --skip publish
  • skipping announce and publish...
  • cleaning distribution directory
  • loading environment variables
  • getting and validating git state
    • couldn't find any tags before "29.27.0"
    • git state                                      commit=552e38f85077118f0d7780131cf1d2cb7d5af8d9 branch=main current_tag=29.27.0 previous_tag=<unknown> dirty=true
  ⨯ release failed after 0s                  error=git is in a dirty state
Please check in your pipeline what can be changing the following files:
?? src/gomodcache/

Learn more at https://goreleaser.com/errors/dirty
The release fails because a new untracked directory `src/gomodcache/` appears during the build, and GoReleaser refuses to run in a dirty git tree. Let me find what creates that directory.

result.output:
1	name: Release
2	
3	on:
4	  push:
5	    branches:
6	      - main
7	    paths:
8	      - "src/**"
9	      - "packages/**"
10	      - ".github/workflows/**"
11	  workflow_dispatch:
12	
13	concurrency:
14	  group: ${{ github.workflow }}
15	
16	jobs:
17	  changelog:
18	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
19	    outputs:
20	      version: ${{ steps.changelog.outputs.version }}
21	      body: ${{ steps.changelog.outputs.clean_changelog }}
22	      tag: ${{ steps.changelog.outputs.tag }}
23	      skipped: ${{ steps.changelog.outputs.skipped }}
24	    steps:
25	      - name: Checkout code 👋
26	        uses: actions/checkout@9c091bb21b7c1c1d1991bb908d89e4e9dddfe3e0
27	      - name: Create changelog ✍️
28	        id: changelog
29	        uses: TriPSs/conventional-changelog-action@ee43def3241429afddab215ba89090cbf64fb45c
30	        with:
31	          github-token: ${{ secrets.github_token }}
32	          skip-version-file: "true"
33	          output-file: "false"
34	          skip-commit: "true"
35	          skip-on-empty: "true"
36	          skip-tag: "true"
37	
38	  android:
39	    needs: changelog
40	    if: ${{ needs.changelog.outputs.skipped == 'false' }}
41	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
42	    container: ghcr.io/jandedobbeleer/golang-android-container:latest
43	    steps:
44	      - name: Checkout code 👋
45	        uses: actions/checkout@9c091bb21b7c1c1d1991bb908d89e4e9dddfe3e0
46	      - name: Build 🏗
47	        run: |
48	          cd src
49	          go build -o dist/posh-android-arm -ldflags="-s -w -X 'github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/build.Version=${{ needs.changelog.outputs.version }}' -X 'github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/build.Date=$(date)'"
50	      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@043fb46d1a93c77aae656e7c1c64a875d1fc6a0a
51	        with:
52	          name: android-artifact
53	          path: src/dist/posh-android-arm
54	
55	  artifacts:
56	    needs: changelog
57	    if: ${{ needs.changelog.outputs.skipped == 'false' }}
58	    runs-on: windows-latest
59	    defaults:
60	      run:
61	        shell: pwsh
62	        working-directory: ${{ github.workspace }}/build
63	    steps:
64	      - name: Checkout code 👋
65	        uses: actions/checkout@9c091bb21b7c1c1d1991bb908d89e4e9dddfe3e0
66	      - name: Install Go 🗳
67	        uses: ./.github/workflows/composite/bootstrap-go
68	      - name: Pre Build 😸
69	        env:
70	          SIGNING_KEY: ${{ secrets.SIGNING_KEY }}
71	        run: |
72	          ./pre.ps1 -Version ${{ needs.changelog.outputs.version }} -SDKVersion "10.0.26100.0"
73	      - name: Package Go module dependencies 📦
74	        working-directory: ${{ github.workspace }}/src
75	        run: |
76	          $env:GOMODCACHE = Join-Path $PWD "gomodcache"
77	          go mod download -modcacherw
78	          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path dist | Out-Null
79	          tar -acf "dist/oh-my-posh-${{ needs.changelog.outputs.version }}-deps.tar.xz" gomodcache
80	      - name: Run GoReleaser 🚀
81	        uses: goreleaser/goreleaser-action@f06c13b6b1a9625abc9e6e439d9c05a8f2190e94
82	        with:
83	          distribution: goreleaser
84	          version: v2.3.2
85	          args: release --clean --skip publish
86	          workdir: src
87	        env:
88	          AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET: ${{ secrets.AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET }}
89	          AZURE_CLIENT_ID: ${{ secrets.AZURE_CLIENT_ID }}
90	          AZURE_TENANT_ID: ${{ secrets.AZURE_TENANT_ID }}
91	      - name: Post Build 🤐
92	        run: |
93	          ./post.ps1
94	      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@043fb46d1a93c77aae656e7c1c64a875d1fc6a0a
95	        with:
96	          name: build-artifacts
97	          path: |
98	            src/dist/posh-*
99	            src/dist/themes.*
100	            src/dist/checksums.*
101	            src/dist/*-deps.tar.xz
102	  msix:
103	    needs:
104	      - changelog
105	      - artifacts
106	    runs-on: windows-latest
107	    strategy:
108	      matrix:
109	        arch: [x64, arm64]
110	    defaults:
111	      run:
112	        shell: pwsh
113	        working-directory: ${{ github.workspace }}/packages/msix
114	    steps:
115	      - name: Checkout code 👋
116	        uses: actions/checkout@9c091bb21b7c1c1d1991bb908d89e4e9dddfe3e0
117	      - uses: actions/download-artifact@3e5f45b2cfb9172054b4087a40e8e0b5a5461e7c
118	        with:
119	          name: build-artifacts
120	          path: dist
121	      - name: Build installer 📦
122	        id: build
123	        env:
124	          AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET: ${{ secrets.AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET }}
125	          AZURE_CLIENT_ID: ${{ secrets.AZURE_CLIENT_ID }}
126	          AZURE_TENANT_ID: ${{ secrets.AZURE_TENANT_ID }}
127	        run: |
128	          $version = '${{ needs.changelog.outputs.version }}'.TrimStart("v")
129	          ./build.ps1 -Architecture ${{ matrix.arch }}  -Version $version -Copy -Sign -SDKVersion "10.0.26100.0"
130	      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@043fb46d1a93c77aae656e7c1c64a875d1fc6a0a
131	        with:
132	          name: msix-artifact-${{ matrix.arch }}
133	          path: |
134	            packages/msix/out/install-${{ matrix.arch }}.msix
135	  release:
136	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
137	    needs:
138	      - changelog
139	      - artifacts
140	      - msix
141	      - android
142	    steps:
143	      - uses: actions/download-artifact@3e5f45b2cfb9172054b4087a40e8e0b5a5461e7c
144	        with:
145	          merge-multiple: true
146	      - name: Upload version file
147	        env:
148	          AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING: ${{ secrets.CDN_CONNECTIONSTRING }}
149	        run: |
150	          echo v${{ needs.changelog.outputs.version }} > version.txt
151	          az storage blob upload-batch --destination releases/v${{ needs.changelog.outputs.version }} --source .
152	          az storage blob upload-batch --destination releases/latest --overwrite true --source .
153	      - name: Create draft release 📝
154	        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@718ea10b132b3b2eba29c1007bb80653f286566b
155	        with:
156	          tag_name: ${{ needs.changelog.outputs.tag }}
157	          body: ${{ needs.changelog.outputs.body }}
158	          fail_on_unmatched_files: true
159	          draft: true
160	          token: ${{ secrets.GH_PAT }}
161	          files: |
162	            *
163	      - name: Publish release 🎓
164	        env:
165	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GH_PAT }}
166	        run: gh release edit ${{ needs.changelog.outputs.tag }} --draft=false --repo ${{ github.repository }}
167	  winget:
168	    runs-on: windows-latest
169	    needs:
170	      - changelog
171	      - release
172	    env:
173	      WINGETCREATE_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.WINGETCREATE_TOKEN }}
174	    steps:
175	    - name: Create manifest and submit PR 📦
176	      shell: pwsh
177	      run: |
178	        Write-Host "Preparing to submit to WinGet repository..." -ForegroundColor Green
179	
180	        # Install the latest wingetcreate exe
181	        # Need to do things this way, see https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell/issues/13138
182	        Write-Verbose "Importing Appx module using Windows PowerShell compatibility"
183	        Import-Module Appx -UseWindowsPowerShell -ErrorAction Stop
184	
185	        # Download and install Winget-Create msixbundle
186	        $appxBundleFile = Join-Path -Path $env:TEMP -ChildPath "wingetcreate.msixbundle"
187	        Write-Verbose "Downloading wingetcreate to: $appxBundleFile"
188	
189	        Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "https://aka.ms/wingetcreate/latest/msixbundle" -OutFile $appxBundleFile -ErrorAction Stop
190	        Add-AppxPackage -Path $appxBundleFile -ErrorAction Stop
191	
192	        Write-Verbose "Successfully installed wingetcreate"
193	
194	        # Submit the PR to WinGet repository
195	        Write-Host "Submitting pull request to WinGet repository..." -ForegroundColor Green
196	
197	        $version = "${{ needs.changelog.outputs.tag }}"
198	        $version = $version.TrimStart('v')
199	
200	        $urls = @(
201	            "https://github.com/JanDeDobbeleer/oh-my-posh/releases/download/v$version/install-x64.msix|x64",
202	            "https://github.com/JanDeDobbeleer/oh-my-posh/releases/download/v$version/install-arm64.msix|arm64"
203	        )
204	
205	        wingetcreate update JanDeDobbeleer.OhMyPosh --version $version --token $env:WINGETCREATE_TOKEN --submit --urls $urls
206

input.command: mkdir -p src/gomodcache && git status --porcelain && git check-ignore -v src/gomodcache && rmdir src/gomodcache

content.text:
Fixed — one line in [.gitignore:184](.gitignore:184).

**What broke:** The recently added "Package Go module dependencies" step in [release.yml:76](.github/workflows/release.yml:76) sets `GOMODCACHE` to `src/gomodcache` so it can tar the dependencies into the release artifacts. That directory stays behind as untracked, and GoReleaser's git-state validation counts untracked files as a dirty tree, so the release step right after it fails.

**The fix:** GoReleaser's dirty check honors `.gitignore`, so I added `/src/gomodcache` next to the existing `/src/dist` entry in the "go releaser" section. I verified with `git check-ignore` that the directory is now ignored while creating it leaves the tree clean.

The alternative would have been pointing `GOMODCACHE` at `$env:RUNNER_TEMP` and taring with `-C`, but the gitignore entry is simpler, matches how `src/dist` is already handled, and also keeps the tree clean for anyone running the packaging step locally.

The change is uncommitted on `next`. Note the workflow releases from `main`, so it needs to land there before the next release run.
still an issue here https://github.com/JanDeDobbeleer/oh-my-posh/actions/runs/29260087477/job/86850737756 should we just moved the part AFTER we build the binaries?
Let me look at the actual failure in that run first.

input.new_string:
$env:GOMODCACHE = Join-Path $env:RUNNER_TEMP "gomodcache"
          go mod download -modcacherw
          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path dist | Out-Null
          tar -acf "dist/oh-my-posh-${{ needs.changelog.outputs.version }}-deps.tar.xz" -C $env:RUNNER_TEMP gomodcache

input.description: Simulate the tar -C invocation in scratchpad

content.text:
That's the fix, verified locally: the archive still lands in `src/dist/` and its entries are still rooted at `gomodcache/`, identical layout to before — only the cache directory itself now lives in the runner's temp dir instead of inside the module.

To answer your question directly: moving the step after GoReleaser would also have worked (the dirty check and the `go mod tidy` hook both run before the builds), but it just hides the conflict — the module cache inside the module tree is what made `go mod tidy` enumerate every cached dependency as if it were your own package. With the cache in `$env:RUNNER_TEMP`, the step can stay where it is and nothing else in the pipeline can trip over it.

The change in [release.yml:76-79](.github/workflows/release.yml:76-79) is uncommitted on `next`. The `/src/gomodcache` entry in .gitignore is now redundant for CI, but I'd leave it — it keeps the tree clean for anyone who runs the old packaging command locally.
but I'm on main here, I added it nromally.
Let me check the current state then.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: D:\oh-my-posh\.claude\skills\conventional-commit

# Conventional Commit

## Commit Message Structure

```text
<type>(<scope>): <description>

[optional body]

[optional footer(s)]
```

### Types

| Type       | When to use                                            |
| ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| `feat`     | A new feature                                          |
| `fix`      | A bug fix                                              |
| `docs`     | Documentation changes, no code                         |
| `style`    | Formatting, missing semicolons, etc. (no logic change) |
| `refactor` | Code change that is neither a fix nor a feature        |
| `perf`     | Performance improvement                                |
| `test`     | Adding or correcting tests                             |
| `ci`       | CI configuration changes                               |
| `chore`    | Maintenance tasks (updating deps, tooling, etc.)       |
| `revert`   | Reverts a previous commit                              |

Append `!` after the type/scope to signal a **breaking change**: `feat!:` or `feat(api)!:`
When a change breaks existing behavior, **both markers are mandatory**: the `!` suffix on the type
**and** the `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer. They always appear together — never one without the other.

### Scope

Optional. Use the name of the area affected, e.g., `segment`, `cache`, `config`, `ui`.
Omit when the change is truly cross-cutting.

### Description

- Required. One short imperative sentence, no period at the end. The full header line (type +
  scope + description) must be **72 characters or fewer**. Aim for **50 characters or fewer for
  the description itself** — this almost always keeps the full header within budget regardless
  of type and scope length.
- Use the imperative mood: "add", not "added" or "adds". Never past tense or present-third-person:
  ✗ `added`, `fixed`, `bumped`, `implemented` → ✓ `add`, `fix`, `bump`, `implement`.
- **Never mirror the input's phrasing.** If the request uses past-tense words (`updated`, `added`,
  `bumped`, `was removed`, `got regenerated`), convert them to imperative before writing the
  description: `update`, `add`, `bump`, `remove`, `regenerate`.

### Body

Optional. Add context about _why_ the change was made, not _what_. The diff shows that.
Wrap at 72 characters.

### Footer

Use for:

- `BREAKING CHANGE: <description>` (required when `!` is used; explains the break).
- Issue references: `Closes #123`, `Fixes #456`.
- Co-authors: `Co-Authored-By: Name <email>`.

## Workflow

1. Run `git status` to review changed files.
2. Run `git diff` and `git diff --cached` to inspect staged and unstaged changes.
3. Identify the **type** from the table above. Ask yourself: does this change **remove, rename, or
   alter existing behavior** that callers depend on? If yes → it is a breaking change: use `!`
   after the type/scope **and** add a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer. Both markers are always required
   together.
4. Identify the **scope** from the files/area changed.
5. Write a short **description** in the imperative mood.
6. Add a **body** if the _why_ needs explanation.
7. Add a **footer** for breaking changes or issue references.
8. Stage the relevant files explicitly (avoid `git add -A`).
9. Commit with a message that preserves multi-line formatting when body/footer are present.

## Examples

```text
feat(segment): add Ramadan segment with Aladhan API
fix(cache): always store mod time
docs(readme): update installation instructions
refactor(config): simplify option parsing logic
chore(deps): bump github.com/shirou/gopsutil/v4
feat(segment)!: rename template property StartTime to Start

BREAKING CHANGE: template strings using .StartTime must be updated to .Start
```

## Validation Checklist

- [ ] Type is one of the allowed values in .commitlintrc.yml
- [ ] The commit message respects the rules defined in .commitlintrc.yml
- [ ] Scope (if present) reflects the actual area changed
- [ ] Description is imperative mood, no trailing period
- [ ] Full header line (type + scope + description) is 72 characters or fewer
- [ ] Both `!` after type/scope **and** `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer are present whenever the change breaks existing behavior
- [ ] No sensitive files staged (.env, credentials, etc.)

input.command:
git add .github/workflows/release.yml && git commit -m "fix(ci): keep Go module cache outside the module tree

Downloading modules into src/gomodcache made GoReleaser's
'go mod tidy' hook scan the cache as if it were part of the
module. Use RUNNER_TEMP for the cache and tar it from there.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
input.description: Commit release workflow fix on main
